title: EuroCropsML Few-Shot Crop-Type Time-Series Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Scientific Data paper and official docs point to the Zenodo release.
Use for dataset construction and benchmark semantics.

instructions: Use the official GitHub package or Zenodo release rather than rebuilding from raw country shapefiles.
Record country, region, parcel ID, time stamps, spectral bands, crop label hierarchy, split, and version.
Start with one or two countries/regions when running iterative SPECTRA exploration.

scale.download_size: large but analysis-ready remote-sensing time-series benchmark; subset by country/region first

recommended_axes.name: geographic_region_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Country, region, and geographic distance from training parcels.

recommended_axes.name: crop_taxonomy_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Crop class hierarchy or semantic distance between crop labels.

recommended_axes.name: phenological_time_series_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity in seasonal spectral trajectories or time-series embeddings.

recommended_axes.name: label_prior_shift
recommended_axes.definition: Distance in crop-class prevalence across countries or regions.

useful_for: Geospatial/time-series SPECTRA audits beyond WILDS PovertyMap.
Testing transnational transfer and few-shot crop-type generalization.
Exploring whether temporal trajectory similarity or geography better explains performance decay.
leakage_risks: Nearby parcels and duplicated geometries can leak spatial context.
Crop label taxonomies differ across countries and may require harmonization.
Class-prior shift can masquerade as representation failure.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified EuroCropsML as a ready-to-use Sentinel-2 crop-type benchmark for transnational/few-shot transfer; official sources confirm GitHub and Zenodo access.
